name_portability: Codename is configurable and swappable; internal packages remain product-neutral.

contract_boundary.rule: Never create local replacement Lattice protos; place extensions in local files, sidecar state, manifests, reports, or UI-internal read models.

sources.title: Building with Lattice
sources.notes: Defines apps, integrations, data services; Entities, Tasks, Objects; REST/gRPC protocol split.

sources.title: Entities overview
sources.notes: Defines entities as component bags powering the COP; asset/track/geo-entity shapes and lifecycle.

sources.title: Tasks overview
sources.notes: Defines deliberate sequential tasks, TaskCatalog, lifecycle, routing, status updates.

sources.title: Lattice Developer Console changelog
sources.notes: Lists Developer Console features: Entity Explorer, Task Explorer, Object Explorer, schemas, /developer-console and /c2.

sources.title: Migrate to v2
sources.notes: Confirms REST/gRPC availability and Buf-generated gRPC artifacts.

sources.title: Lattice Command & Control
sources.notes: Public lane for C2/common operating picture and battle-management positioning.

sources.title: Lattice Mission Autonomy
sources.notes: Public lane for mission autonomy and human-supervised multi-asset autonomy.

sources.title: Lattice Mesh
sources.notes: Public lane for tactical-edge data distribution and mesh-like workflows.

sources.title: Lattice SDK
sources.notes: Public lane for SDK apps, data services, and hardware integrations.

sources.title: Lattice Partner Program
sources.notes: Public lane for partner resources, sandboxes, validation, and reference applications.

lanes.name: Compatibility Kernel
lanes.mission: Keep public-compatible REST/gRPC behavior stable while all higher-level Zorn modules expand around it.

lanes.name: Sandbox Environment Manager
lanes.mission: Provide resettable, seedable, replayable local environments that resemble developer sandboxes.

lanes.name: Developer Console
lanes.mission: Expose a debugging console for entities, tasks, objects, streams, schemas, and compatibility status.

lanes.name: Operator C2
lanes.mission: Build a map-first COP and tasking surface for rich showcase scenarios without copying proprietary UI design.

lanes.name: Sensors, Tracks, and Protocol Adapters
lanes.mission: Translate simulation and open feed protocols into public-compatible entities, tasks, objects, and events.

lanes.name: Objects, Media, Reports, and Evidence
lanes.mission: Turn the Objects API into visible workflows: thumbnails, reports, manifests, scenario evidence, and exports.

lanes.name: Mission Autonomy and Taskable Agents
lanes.mission: Exercise TaskCatalog routing, multi-agent tasking, human-supervised mission graphs, and mock autonomy.

lanes.name: Mesh and Edge Simulation
lanes.mission: Simulate local-first event distribution, object availability, partitions, reconnection, and constrained links.

lanes.name: Partner-Style Integration System
lanes.mission: Provide a local partner sandbox experience for packages, manifests, conformance tests, and compatibility scorecards.

lanes.name: Domain Scenario Packs
lanes.mission: Package rich but safe demonstrations across maritime, DIS, C-UAS surveillance, space tracking, undersea, and readiness workflows.

lanes.name: Showcase and Evaluation Mode
lanes.mission: Make the whole environment demonstrable, repeatable, measurable, and presentable.

milestones.name: Sandbox Environment Manager
milestones.objective: Introduce first-class local environments with isolation, deterministic time, seed/reset/export, fixture packs, and scenario bootstrap files and commands.
milestones.why_now: Every rich showcase needs repeatability. Environment management prevents the C2 UI, sample app tests, adapters, and mesh simulation from fighting over shared state.

epics.name: Environment registry
stories: Create/list/get/delete local environment records.
Attach environment_id to entities, tasks, objects, and event log rows without breaking existing default behavior.
Expose active environment selection through startup configuration and UI-local files.

epics.name: Seed, reset, export, and import
stories: Add seed packs for empty, AIS demo, DIS demo, C-UAS surveillance demo, and autonomy patrol demo.
Implement deterministic reset that clears current state and replays seed data.
Export environment state as jsonl plus object metadata manifest.

epics.name: Deterministic clock
stories: Add real-time and simulated-time modes.
Allow pause/resume/speed multiplier for scenarios.
Ensure TTL/expiry, task status timestamps, and event ordering can use simulated time.

epics.name: Fixture and schema pack manager
stories: Register fixture packs with metadata, source, safety classification, and supported scenarios.
Validate fixture packs before loading.
Expose fixture pack status in Developer Console.

deliverables: Environment manifests and CLI inspection commands.
acceptance_tests: Resetting the same seed twice yields the same entity/task/object/event counts and ordered event hashes.
Two environments can contain the same entityId without state collision.
Simulated time pause prevents expiry and task timeline advancement.
Exported environment can be imported into a clean database and pass equality checks.
milestones.showcase_signal: A user can pick a scenario pack, reset it, run it, pause time, and export the resulting state for debugging.

milestones.name: Developer Console MVP
milestones.objective: Build the first web console for inspecting, filtering, validating, and debugging entities, tasks, objects, streams, schemas, and compatibility results.
milestones.why_now: The console becomes the workspace for contract hardening, sample-app debugging, adapter development, and scenario authoring.

epics.name: Entity Explorer
stories: Browse/filter/search entities by template, disposition, environment, source, liveness, and last update.
Open entity detail with component cards, raw JSON, provenance, and event history.
Validate selected entity against known public-compatible schema checks and local lint rules.

epics.name: Task Explorer
stories: Browse tasks by status, assignee, task type URL, creator, and terminal/non-terminal state.
Show task lifecycle timeline, status payloads, errors, progress, result, and relation graph.
Expose manual create/status/cancel actions in dev mode.

epics.name: Object Explorer
stories: Browse objects by path prefix, size, checksum, expiry, and linked entity.
Preview supported images/text/json and download raw objects.
Show object upload/delete events and TTL status.

epics.name: Stream and compatibility inspector
stories: Display active SSE/gRPC streams, heartbeat cadence, event counts, reconnects, and last error.
Render descriptor audit and sample-app compatibility status.
Export debug bundle with state snapshot, event log tail, and compatibility report.

acceptance_tests: Entity Explorer displays seeded asset, track, and geo-entity examples.
Task Explorer displays Create → Update → Cancel/Complete timeline.
Object Explorer previews a thumbnail object and shows metadata.
Stream Inspector shows entity stream heartbeats and live create/update/delete events.
milestones.showcase_signal: A developer can explain exactly why the COP looks the way it does by drilling from UI object to entity component to event to source integration.
non_goals: No pixel clone of any proprietary UI.

milestones.name: Operator C2 MVP
milestones.objective: Create a map-first operator view that turns live entities, tasks, objects, and streams into a believable common operational picture.
milestones.why_now: This is the first milestone that makes Zorn feel like a rich environment rather than a backend test server.

epics.name: Map-first COP shell
stories: Render entities with location on a map using template/disposition/environment-derived symbology.
Display trails, headings, speed, altitude/depth, and stale/live/deleted state.
Support layer toggles for assets, tracks, geo-entities, trails, coverage, labels, and tasks.

epics.name: Object list and selection
stories: List assets, tracks, geo-entities, alerts, and active tasks in side rail.
Support search and filters aligned with Entity Explorer.
Show selected entity detail with core telemetry, provenance, media, and task actions.

epics.name: Task board and operator actions
stories: Show pending/assigned/in-progress/completed/failed/canceled task lanes.
Create allowed mock tasks against selected assets/tracks/areas.
Cancel tasks and show status timeline updates in the side panel.

epics.name: Alerts and watchlists
stories: Create local watchlist rules for stale asset, unknown track near zone, task failure, and object expiry.
Render non-kinetic alerts in map/list panels.
Allow operator acknowledgement as local UI state.

deliverables: Map renderer with live entity stream.
Selected entity details with task/media panels.
Task board and basic alert drawer.
acceptance_tests: AIS or synthetic entity replay moves icons on the map through entity stream updates.
Selecting an entity highlights it in the map, list, and detail panel.
Creating a mock investigation task shows it on the task board and routes it to a mock agent.
Alert rule triggers on a seeded unknown track near a configured training zone.
milestones.showcase_signal: An observer sees a live, explainable COP with moving tracks, selected details, tasking, alerts, and media attachments.
non_goals: No real-world targeting, engagement recommendation, or weapon-release workflow.
No copying proprietary Anduril visual identity.

milestones.name: Sensor, Track, and Protocol Adapters
milestones.objective: Build adapter infrastructure and initial AIS/DIS/HLA/CoT-style ingest paths that feed public-compatible entities/events into Zorn.
milestones.why_now: Adapters make the environment look alive and prove Zorn can serve as a simulation integration platform.

epics.name: Adapter framework
stories: Define adapter lifecycle: configure, validate, start, pause, stop, health, metrics.
Add adapter-to-entity mapping registry with source/provenance rules.
Expose adapter health and throughput in Developer Console.

epics.name: AIS adapter
stories: Convert AIS vessel fixtures into surface track/entity updates.
Map MMSI/callsign/name into aliases and provenance.
Support replay speed and deterministic event order.

epics.name: DIS Entity State adapter
stories: Map DIS entity identifiers to stable entityId/aliases.
Map DIS location, velocity, orientation, force ID, domain, and entity type into compatible entity components.
Add namespace policy for exercise ID and site/application/entity IDs.

epics.name: Sensor and detection modeling
stories: Represent generic sensor coverage as geo shapes and sensor components.
Represent detections as tracks or source-linked entity updates.
Preserve source confidence/quality as tracked/provenance metadata when available.

deliverables: Adapter plugin base classes and manifests.
DIS Entity State PDU mapping skeleton.
acceptance_tests: AIS replay creates moving surface tracks visible in /c2 and /developer-console.
DIS fixture creates asset/track entities with stable IDs and deterministic provenance.
Adapter failure is surfaced as health status without crashing the server.
Replay can pause/resume through the sandbox clock.
milestones.showcase_signal: External/simulated data feeds become live COP objects with traceable mappings and health telemetry.
non_goals: No live unapproved network ingest by default.

milestones.name: Objects, Media, Reports, and Evidence Workflows
milestones.objective: Turn object storage into visible workflows for thumbnails, reports, manifests, sensor captures, replay artifacts, and exportable evidence bundles.
milestones.why_now: Media and reports make demos richer and exercise the full Entities + Objects relationship rather than entity telemetry alone.

epics.name: Object metadata hardening
stories: Add checksum/size/content-type/expiry/path-prefix query behavior to tests.
Support object lifecycle events for upload/delete/expiry.
Represent object-to-entity linkage in local indexes without altering public object API.

epics.name: Thumbnail and media flow
stories: Upload image object and attach it to selected entity media component/override where compatible.
Preview image and basic text/json content in entity detail and object browser.
Generate synthetic thumbnails for seeded tracks/assets.

epics.name: Reports and evidence bundles
stories: Generate scenario report object with entity/task/event summaries.
Export evidence bundle with objects, event log, task timeline, and compatibility report.
Add redaction hooks for sensitive/local-only fields.

epics.name: Object distribution simulation hooks
stories: Add local object availability metadata to support later mesh simulation.
Track which simulated nodes have each object.
Expose object distribution state as Zorn-only metadata in UI.

acceptance_tests: Uploading a thumbnail object makes it visible on selected entity detail.
Deleting an object updates browser state and event log.
Evidence export contains manifest, events, tasks, objects, and checksums.
Object metadata remains REST-compatible while local distribution metadata remains namespaced.
milestones.showcase_signal: A demo can show track thumbnails, generated reports, downloadable object artifacts, and a complete scenario evidence package.
non_goals: No sensitive media processing by default.

milestones.name: Mission Autonomy and Taskable Agents
milestones.objective: Build mock agents and mission graph execution to exercise task catalogs, task routing, status updates, cancellation, and human-supervised autonomy.
milestones.why_now: Taskable agents make the task API meaningful and enable richer multi-asset demos without needing real robotics.

epics.name: Agent registry and TaskCatalog matching
stories: Register mock agents as entities with TaskCatalog components.
Match task specification type URLs to capable agents.
Render agent capability and last heartbeat in Developer Console and /c2.

epics.name: Mock agent runtime
stories: Implement agent loop: listen, accept, update progress, complete/fail/cancel.
Support deterministic behavior under sandbox clock.
Publish agent telemetry and status entities while executing tasks.

epics.name: Mission graph DSL
stories: Define local YAML/JSON mission graph with tasks, dependencies, gates, and rollback/cancel behavior.
Add human approval gates before simulated task execution where appropriate.
Render mission graph timeline in UI.

epics.name: Agent outputs
stories: Agents can publish new entity observations, task status, and object attachments.
Camera-like agent can attach synthetic thumbnail/report objects.
Failure modes generate errors visible in task timeline.

acceptance_tests: A task created in /c2 routes to a matching mock agent via ListenAsAgent.
Agent status progresses through sent/accepted/in-progress/complete or failed.
Cancel request interrupts task and records terminal status.
Mission graph pauses at human approval gate and resumes only after operator approval.
milestones.showcase_signal: The environment demonstrates human-supervised multi-agent task execution with visible task lifecycles and agent-generated outputs.

milestones.name: Mesh and Edge Simulation
milestones.objective: Simulate distributed edge behavior: node-local event logs, constrained links, partitions, reconnect, object availability, and deterministic reconciliation.
milestones.why_now: Mesh-like behavior distinguishes Zorn from a single-node mock and lets demos show tactical-edge constraints safely.

epics.name: Node registry and topology
stories: Define simulated nodes with roles, location, storage, and connectivity profile.
Render topology graph in Developer Console.
Allow scenario packs to declare node topology.

epics.name: Event replication simulator
stories: Replicate entity/task/object events between nodes according to link profiles.
Queue events during partitions and replay on reconnect.
Expose replication lag and conflict resolution trace.

epics.name: Object distribution simulator
stories: Track object availability per node.
Simulate object fetch failures when partitioned or unavailable.
Surface object distribution state in Object Explorer and C2 media panel.

epics.name: Degraded-link profiles
stories: Support latency, bandwidth, packet loss, and offline profiles.
Apply link profiles to streams and object transfers in simulated mode.
Add UI controls to toggle network impairment during demos.

acceptance_tests: Partitioning a node stops remote event visibility while local state continues.
Reconnect replays queued events in deterministic order.
Object unavailable on a node shows a clear simulated fetch failure.
Replication trace explains how a final entity state was chosen after conflict.
milestones.showcase_signal: A demo can disconnect an edge node, continue local operations, then reconnect and show deterministic reconciliation.
non_goals: No proprietary Lattice Mesh implementation claim.

milestones.name: Partner-Style Integration System
milestones.objective: Create local partner-style integration workflows: package manifests, capability catalogs, conformance suites, reference datasets, and scorecards.
milestones.why_now: The partner lane turns Zorn into a showcase platform where adapters/apps/agents can be installed, evaluated, and compared.

epics.name: Integration package manifest
stories: Define package types: app, adapter, agent, data-service, scenario-pack, domain-pack.
Declare inputs, outputs, API dependencies, task definitions, objects, permissions, and safety notes.
Validate manifests and show errors before installation.

epics.name: Capability catalog
stories: Index installed integrations by capabilities and supported scenarios.
Show compatibility status with REST, gRPC, sample apps, and scenario packs.
Render integration health and recent events.

epics.name: Conformance runner
stories: Run package-specific tests against a clean sandbox.
Generate scorecard: API calls exercised, events emitted, objects created, tasks routed.
Export scorecard as JSON and human-readable report.

epics.name: Reference data packs
stories: Bundle known-good fixtures for AIS, DIS, media, tasks, and mesh partitions.
Declare data provenance and safety boundaries.
Use reference packs in CI and showcase mode.

acceptance_tests: A sample AIS adapter package installs and declares entity-publishing capability.
A mock agent package installs and declares TaskCatalog support.
Conformance runner executes package tests in a reset sandbox.
Scorecard accurately reports pass/fail and exercised capabilities.
milestones.showcase_signal: Zorn can act as a local partner sandbox where integrations are discovered, installed, tested, and scored.
non_goals: No claim of official Anduril partner validation.
No execution of untrusted plugin code without explicit local opt-in.

milestones.name: Domain Scenario Packs
milestones.objective: Package rich, safe, repeatable domain showcases that exercise C2, adapters, autonomy, objects, and mesh behavior.
milestones.why_now: Domain packs are the showcase surface: they demonstrate why the platform exists and provide regression scenarios for development.

epics.name: Maritime AIS + media pack
stories: Replay AIS tracks with vessel identities and routes.
Attach synthetic manifest/thumbnail objects.
Trigger watchlist alerts for training-only rule examples.

epics.name: DIS multi-domain replay pack
stories: Replay air/surface/ground/subsurface simulated entities.
Show adapter mapping from raw sim identifiers to public-compatible components.
Exercise stream and UI performance with dense tracks.

epics.name: C-UAS surveillance and investigation pack
stories: Represent sensors, surveillance zones, unknown tracks, and investigation tasks safely.
Route non-kinetic investigation task to mock sensor/camera agent.
Generate synthetic report object after task completion.

epics.name: Space, undersea, and readiness packs
stories: Create high-altitude/space-track mock entities with uncertainty and sparse updates.
Create undersea sparse-comms scenario with delayed entity updates and mesh partition behavior.
Create asset readiness checklist workflow with automated/mock readings and human signoff.

deliverables: C-UAS surveillance scenario pack with safety constraints.
acceptance_tests: Each domain pack can be reset, run, paused, exported, and replayed.
Each pack exercises at least three of: Entities, Tasks, Objects, UI, adapters, agents, mesh.
C-UAS pack contains no engagement recommendation, targeting, weapon-release, or fire-control workflow.
All packs generate a scenario report object and event summary.
milestones.showcase_signal: A reviewer can choose from multiple realistic-looking but safe scenarios that show the breadth of the ecosystem.
non_goals: No classified data or controlled datasets.

milestones.name: Rich Showcase Bundle and Evaluation Mode
milestones.objective: Create a polished, repeatable demo/evaluation experience that launches Zorn, runs scenarios, records evidence, and produces reports.
milestones.why_now: Once features exist, the project needs a clean story that proves the ecosystem in one command and generates artifacts for review.

epics.name: One-command showcase runner
stories: Start REST, gRPC, UI, agents, adapters, mesh sim, and selected scenario pack.
Check dependency health before scenario starts.
Provide deterministic cleanup/reset between runs.

epics.name: Evaluation metrics
stories: Record entities published, tasks created/routed/completed, objects created, stream reconnects, mesh lag, and compatibility results.
Emit machine-readable metrics JSON.
Render human-readable evaluation report.

epics.name: Demo recordings and narrative runbooks
stories: Capture API transcripts and UI state snapshots during scenario execution.
Create operator, developer, partner, and evaluator runbooks.
Document expected observations and success signals for each demo.

epics.name: Release readiness and packaging
stories: Package docker-compose local stack.
Add versioned fixture/data bundles.
Generate compatibility matrix and limitation list for each release.

acceptance_tests: A fresh clone can run the showcase bundle and produce a report without manual data setup.
Evaluation report includes compatibility, scenario, UI, adapter, agent, object, and mesh sections.
Each demo has a runbook with expected operator/developer observations.
Release matrix lists supported, partial, and intentionally unsupported features.
milestones.showcase_signal: Zorn can be demonstrated as a complete local environment with API compatibility, COP UI, adapters, agents, mesh simulation, partner integration, and scenario reports.
non_goals: No claim of official Lattice certification.

acceptance_tests.description: Resetting the same seed twice yields the same entity/task/object/event counts and ordered event hashes.
Two environments can contain the same entityId without state collision.
Simulated time pause prevents expiry and task timeline advancement.
Exported environment can be imported into a clean database and pass equality checks.
Entity Explorer displays seeded asset, track, and geo-entity examples.
Task Explorer displays Create → Update → Cancel/Complete timeline.
Object Explorer previews a thumbnail object and shows metadata.
Stream Inspector shows entity stream heartbeats and live create/update/delete events.
AIS or synthetic entity replay moves icons on the map through entity stream updates.
Selecting an entity highlights it in the map, list, and detail panel.
Creating a mock investigation task shows it on the task board and routes it to a mock agent.
Alert rule triggers on a seeded unknown track near a configured training zone.
AIS replay creates moving surface tracks visible in /c2 and /developer-console.
DIS fixture creates asset/track entities with stable IDs and deterministic provenance.
Adapter failure is surfaced as health status without crashing the server.
Replay can pause/resume through the sandbox clock.
Uploading a thumbnail object makes it visible on selected entity detail.
Deleting an object updates browser state and event log.
Evidence export contains manifest, events, tasks, objects, and checksums.
Object metadata remains REST-compatible while local distribution metadata remains namespaced.
A task created in /c2 routes to a matching mock agent via ListenAsAgent.
Agent status progresses through sent/accepted/in-progress/complete or failed.
Cancel request interrupts task and records terminal status.
Mission graph pauses at human approval gate and resumes only after operator approval.
Partitioning a node stops remote event visibility while local state continues.
Reconnect replays queued events in deterministic order.
Object unavailable on a node shows a clear simulated fetch failure.
Replication trace explains how a final entity state was chosen after conflict.
A sample AIS adapter package installs and declares entity-publishing capability.
A mock agent package installs and declares TaskCatalog support.
Conformance runner executes package tests in a reset sandbox.
Scorecard accurately reports pass/fail and exercised capabilities.
Each domain pack can be reset, run, paused, exported, and replayed.
Each pack exercises at least three of: Entities, Tasks, Objects, UI, adapters, agents, mesh.
C-UAS pack contains no engagement recommendation, targeting, weapon-release, or fire-control workflow.
All packs generate a scenario report object and event summary.
A fresh clone can run the showcase bundle and produce a report without manual data setup.
Evaluation report includes compatibility, scenario, UI, adapter, agent, object, and mesh sections.
Each demo has a runbook with expected operator/developer observations.
Release matrix lists supported, partial, and intentionally unsupported features.

risks.risk: Official public SDK/proto behavior changes faster than the mock implementation.
risks.impact: Compatibility regressions in clients and sample apps.
risks.mitigation: Keep descriptor audit, sample-app harness, and compatibility matrix as release gates.

risks.risk: Zorn could be mistaken for an official or affiliated product.
risks.mitigation: Use name-neutral internals, clear disclaimers, no Anduril marks or proprietary UI replication.

risks.risk: Domain scenarios drift toward operational targeting or weapon employment workflows.
risks.mitigation: Keep C-UAS and defense scenarios limited to surveillance, investigation, reporting, readiness, and non-kinetic tasking.

risks.risk: Zorn-only features leak into public-compatible proto/API layers.
risks.mitigation: Place local UI/plugin/scenario/mesh metadata in sidecar state, local manifests, reports, and UI-internal read models only.

risks.risk: UI polish consumes effort before data/stream/task behavior is reliable.
risks.mitigation: Build Developer Console and acceptance matrix before operator-grade COP polish.

risks.risk: Dense scenario packs overwhelm stream/event/UI performance.
risks.impact: Demos fail under realistic track counts.
risks.mitigation: Add performance budgets and synthetic load tests starting in Z5/Z6.

risks.risk: Generated proto packages or public SDK dependencies are unavailable or unexpectedly changed.
risks.mitigation: Pin versions, audit checksums/descriptors, and maintain documented export/snapshot steps.
